findings.title: Token2022 transfer fee not deducted from deposit amount — systematic undercollateralization
findings.description: When a user deposits Token2022 tokens with transfer fees into a reserve, the program records the full user-supplied `liquidity_amount` as the deposited amount without accounting for the transfer fee. Token2022 tokens with `transfer_fee` extension deduct the fee at transfer time: the reserve receives `amount - fee`, but the collateral minted is calculated from the full `amount`. This causes the program's internal liability ledger (cToken minting) to exceed the actual token balance held by the reserve.
findings.impact: Systematic undercollateralization of all Token2022 reserves with transfer fees. Over time, the gap between recorded deposit value and actual token holdings grows, enabling borrows that exceed true collateral value. A large borrow or liquidation against an inflated reserve can drain it below the recorded liability, harming subsequent depositors and borrowers.
findings.recommendation: Use `token_interface::accessor::amount()` to read the actual token balance delta after the `transfer_checked` CPI in `deposit_reserve_liquidity_transfer`, then use the received amount (not the requested amount) for collateral calculation. Alternatively, read the Token2022 mint's `TransferFeeConfig` extension pre-transfer and compute the post-fee amount before minting collateral.

audit_context.related_exploit: Aug 2024 Kamino Finance hack ($4.7M, TokenExtensions overflow)
audit_context.note: Structurally similar pattern; specific attack vector may differ from Aug 2024 exploit

findings.title: [RESOLVED] U256 BigFraction overflow in health factor calculation
findings.description: The on-chain fraction module has been rewritten. The old `BigFraction` U256 struct is replaced by `fixed::types::U68F60`. All multiplication operations now use `checked_mul()` with `?` error propagation. The `pow_fraction` function (lines 42-48) uses `checked_mul` throughout.
findings.impact: Previously, unguarded U256 multiplication in release mode could silently wrap, causing incorrect health factor and LTV calculations. This has been fixed.
findings.recommendation: N/A — resolved. The on-chain program now uses `fixed::types::U68F60` with checked arithmetic.

audit_context.fix_evidence: fraction.rs line 42-48: checked_mul with ? propagation throughout
